aiDescription.summary: The "Import Miva shipments(Update)" import is used to update shipments in the Miva application. It is triggered by a REST API call to the "/json.mvc" endpoint using the POST method. The import includes a filter to process only records with a non-empty "Miva Shipment ID" and a lookup to transform field values. It has a response transformation defined, but no rules are applied. The import has a preMap hook defined, but no custom logic is applied. Each HTTP request includes only one record, and the success of the import is determined by checking the "success" field in the response body.
aiDescription.detailed:
This import is named "Import Miva shipments(Update)" and it is used to update shipments in the Miva application. The import is triggered by a REST API call to the "/json.mvc" endpoint using the POST method. The request body is a JSON object that includes the "Store_Code" and "Function" fields, as well as the data to be updated.

The import has a filter defined, which checks if the "Miva Shipment ID" field is not empty before processing the record. This filter ensures that only records with a non-empty "Miva Shipment ID" are processed.

The import does not create multiple records from a single parent record (oneToMany is set to false). It does not ignore existing data when updating (ignoreExisting is set to false) and it does not ignore missing data (ignoreMissing is set to false).

The import includes a lookup named "miva_shipcarrier_lookup" which maps the values "UPS" and "FedEx/USPS/More" to "UPS" and "USPS" respectively. This lookup is used to transform the values of a field during the import process. The lookup allows failures and has a default value of an empty string.

The import has a response transformation defined, which is an expression that applies rules to transform the response data. However, in this case, the rules are empty.

The import has a preMap hook defined, but the code for the hook is null, meaning that no custom logic is applied before the mapping process.

The import has a batchSize of 1, meaning that each HTTP request will include only one record. The success of the import is determined by checking the "success" field in the response body, which should have a value of "1" to indicate success.

Overall, this import is used to update shipments in the Miva application by making a POST request to the "/json.mvc" endpoint with the necessary data. It applies a filter to process only records with a non-empty "Miva Shipment ID" and includes a lookup to transform the values of a field.
